bullet_points: The story highlights the Meta-Llama-3-8B model, a significant large language model.
It is featured on Hugging Face Benchmarks, indicating its community relevance and performance tracking.
The associated tags confirm its use in text-generation pipelines using standard ML frameworks (transformers, safetensors).

stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model from Meta.
It is featured on Hugging Face Benchmarks, indicating community interest and performance tracking.
The associated tags confirm its relevance to transformers, text-generation, and the Llama family of models.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML technologies like transformers and pytorch.
The pipeline indicates the model is used for text generation tasks.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance gains.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, optimizing AI workloads on Macs.
The feature is currently available in preview, signaling a push for local, high-performance AI on Apple devices.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs on the user's own device.
This emphasizes a trend toward private, on-device AI capabilities rather than cloud-dependent solutions.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, running locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: This is your laptop… on AI
stories.author: David Pierce
bullet_points: The current tech industry narrative is heavily focused on AI's transformative impact on computing.
Big Tech leaders, exemplified by Nvidia's Jensen Huang, are strongly advocating for AI integration into everyday devices.
The focus is shifting towards fundamentally new ways AI will be utilized within personal laptops.

stories.title: Small modular nuclear reactor reaches criticality in first test
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: A small modular nuclear reactor (SMR) has achieved criticality in its initial test.
The technology comes from a startup named Antares.
The reactor is not yet operational for power generation.

stories.title: S&P 500 rejects SpaceX, also blocking entry for OpenAI and Anthropic
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: S&P 500 is reportedly blocking easy access to capital for SpaceX.
The exclusion also extends to major AI players like OpenAI and Anthropic.
This indicates a potential shift in how large, high-growth, or speculative tech companies gain passive investment.

stories.title: Code2LoRA: Hypernetwork-Generated Adapters for Code Language Models under Software Evolution
stories.author: Pengyu Nie
bullet_points: Code2LoRA introduces a hypernetwork framework to generate repository-specific LoRA adapters for code language models.
This method addresses the limitations of current approaches (RAG/fine-tuning) by injecting repository knowledge without increasing inference-time token overhead.
It offers a scalable and robust solution for code models dealing with complex, evolving software repositories (imports, APIs, conventions).

stories.title: MLEvolve: A Self-Evolving Framework for Automated Machine Learning Algorithm Discovery
stories.author: Lei Bai
bullet_points: Introduces MLEvolve, a new LLM-based framework designed for self-evolving Automated Machine Learning (MLE).
Addresses critical limitations in existing MLE agents, such as information isolation, memoryless search, and poor hierarchical control.
Aims to enable sustained self-evolution for complex, long-horizon tasks like scientific discovery and ML engineering.

stories.title: Code2LoRA: Hypernetwork-Generated Adapters for Code Language Models under Software Evolution
stories.author: Pengyu Nie
bullet_points: Code2LoRA introduces a hypernetwork framework to generate repository-specific LoRA adapters for code language models.
This method addresses the limitation of existing techniques (RAG/fine-tuning) which are costly or brittle when handling large, evolving codebases.
The key advantage is injecting repository knowledge without incurring any inference-time token overhead.

stories.title: MLEvolve: A Self-Evolving Framework for Automated Machine Learning Algorithm Discovery
stories.author: Lei Bai
bullet_points: Introduces MLEvolve, a novel LLM-based framework designed for self-evolving Machine Learning Engineering (MLE).
Addresses critical limitations in existing MLE agents, specifically inter-branch information isolation, memoryless search, and poor hierarchical control.
Aims to enable sustained self-evolution for complex, long-horizon tasks like automated ML algorithm discovery.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 introduces performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically benefiting users on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, offering an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The framework emphasizes local-first operation, allowing AI to run entirely on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating the use of local LLMs.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It facilitates the use of proprietary models (like Claude) alongside open-source models.

stories.title: How a USB-connected speaker can infect a PC without ever being touched
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A USB-connected speaker was found capable of infecting a PC without physical user interaction.
The specific device mentioned is the Sound Blaster Katana V2X.
The seller of the device does not classify the observed behavior as a security vulnerability.

stories.title: HANDOFF: Humanoid Agentic Task-Space Whole-Body Control via Distilled Complementary Teachers
stories.author: Aaron Ames
bullet_points: Introduces HANDOFF, a new framework for whole-body control in humanoid robots.
Addresses the critical challenge of bridging high-level task planning with low-level robot control.
Proposes a compact, explicit, and general interface to replace complex, dense kinematic references.

stories.title: TempoVLA: Learning Speed-Controllable Vision-Language-Action Policies
stories.author: Mingyu Ding
bullet_points: TempoVLA addresses a key limitation in Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models: the inability to control execution speed dynamically.
The research tackles the need for variable speed in robot manipulation, distinguishing between fast transit phases and slow, precise contact stages.
Unlike previous methods that only change between fixed speeds, TempoVLA specifically explores and enables controlled deceleration in VLA policies.

stories.title: Operation-Guided Progressive Human-to-AI Text Transformation Benchmark for Multi-Granularity AI-Text Detection
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: Addresses the gap in current AI-text detection benchmarks by focusing on the process, not just the final output.
Introduces OpAI-Bench, a new operation-guided benchmark designed to study how AI authorship signals evolve during human-AI co-editing.
Relevant to the growing integration of AI writing assistants into real-world drafting and revision workflows.

stories.title: Pretraining Recurrent Networks without Recurrence
stories.author: Phillip Isola
bullet_points: The paper addresses limitations of standard Backpropagation Through Time (BPTT) in training Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs), specifically regarding sequential computation and gradient issues.
It introduces Supervised Memory Training (SMT), a novel method designed to train nonlinear RNNs.
SMT achieves this by completely bypassing the need for recurrent credit propagation, potentially improving parallelism and long-range dependency learning.

stories.title: RREDCoT: Segment-Level Reward Redistribution for Reasoning Models
stories.author: Sepp Hochreiter
bullet_points: The paper addresses the delayed reward problem inherent in Reinforcement Learning (RL) fine-tuning of reasoning language models.
It introduces RREDCoT, a method for Segment-Level Reward Redistribution, to improve the training process.
The work targets limitations in existing RL techniques like GRPO when steering models toward Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.

stories.title: Self-Augmenting Retrieval for Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Kilian Q. Weinberger
bullet_points: Introduces a novel technique, Self-Augmenting Retrieval, for Diffusion Language Models.
Leverages low-confidence tokens generated during the parallel denoising process as a valuable lookahead signal.
This signal enables more effective retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) by surfacing salient entities early in the generation trajectory.

stories.title: PC Layer: Polynomial Weight Preconditioning for Improving LLM Pre-Training
stories.author: Ruoyu Sun
bullet_points: Introduces a Polynomial Weight Preconditioning (PC) layer to stabilize LLM training.
The PC module reshapes the singular-value spectrum of weight matrices using low-degree polynomials.
The method adds no inference overhead as preconditioned weights can be merged back into the original architecture.

stories.title: TailLoR: Protecting Principal Components in Parameter-Efficient Continual Learning
stories.author: Florin Brad
bullet_points: Introduces TailLoR, a new parameter-efficient finetuning method for Continual Learning.
It uses the singular bases (U and V) of pre-trained weights as a fixed reference frame for low-rank updates.
A soft spectral penalty is employed to prevent updates from interfering with dominant singular directions, promoting fine-grained adaptation.

stories.title: HANDOFF: Humanoid Agentic Task-Space Whole-Body Control via Distilled Complementary Teachers
stories.author: Aaron Ames
bullet_points: Introduces HANDOFF, a new framework for whole-body control in humanoid robots.
Addresses the critical challenge of bridging high-level task planning with low-level robot control.
Proposes a compact, explicit, and intuitive interface to replace dense kinematic references.

stories.title: Operation-Guided Progressive Human-to-AI Text Transformation Benchmark for Multi-Granularity AI-Text Detection
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: Addresses the gap in current AI-text detection benchmarks by focusing on the process, not just the final output.
Introduces OpAI-Bench, a new operation-guided benchmark designed to study how AI authorship signals evolve during human-AI co-editing.
Relevant to the growing integration of AI writing assistants into real-world drafting and revision workflows.

stories.title: Pretraining Recurrent Networks without Recurrence
stories.author: Phillip Isola
bullet_points: Addresses limitations of standard Backpropagation Through Time (BPTT) in training Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs).
Proposes Supervised Memory Training (SMT) to bypass the need for recurrent credit propagation.
Aims to overcome issues like sequential processing, vanishing/exploding gradients, and poor long-range association learning in RNNs.

stories.title: RREDCoT: Segment-Level Reward Redistribution for Reasoning Models
stories.author: Sepp Hochreiter
bullet_points: The paper addresses the delayed reward problem inherent in Reinforcement Learning (RL) fine-tuning of reasoning language models.
It introduces RREDCoT, a method for Segment-Level Reward Redistribution, to improve training efficiency.
The work focuses on overcoming limitations of standard RL techniques like GRPO when applied to Chain-of-Thought (CoT) generation.

stories.title: Self-Augmenting Retrieval for Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Kilian Q. Weinberger
bullet_points: The research introduces a method to leverage discarded tokens from Discrete Diffusion Language Models.
These discarded, low-confidence tokens serve as a valuable 'lookahead signal' for retrieval-augmented generation (RAG).
The technique suggests that even tentative tokens during the denoising process can surface salient entities useful for external knowledge retrieval.

stories.title: PC Layer: Polynomial Weight Preconditioning for Improving LLM Pre-Training
stories.author: Ruoyu Sun
bullet_points: Introduces a Polynomial Weight Preconditioning (PC) layer to stabilize LLM training.
The PC module reshapes the singular-value spectrum of weight matrices using low-degree polynomials.
The method allows for merging preconditioned weights back into the original architecture with no inference overhead.

stories.title: Operation-Guided Progressive Human-to-AI Text Transformation Benchmark for Multi-Granularity AI-Text Detection
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: Addresses the gap in current AI-text detection benchmarks, which fail to account for progressive human-AI co-editing.
Introduces OpAI-Bench, a new operation-guided benchmark designed to study how AI authorship signals evolve during revision.
Highlights the growing real-world complexity of documents resulting from continuous human-AI collaboration.

stories.title: Self-Augmenting Retrieval for Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Kilian Q. Weinberger
bullet_points: The research introduces a method to leverage discarded tokens from discrete diffusion language models.
These discarded, low-confidence tokens are shown to act as a useful lookahead signal for retrieval-augmented generation (RAG).
This technique helps in retrieving stronger evidence by capturing salient entities early in the denoising process.

stories.title: You Only Index Once: Cross-Layer Sparse Attention with Shared Routing
stories.author: Furu Wei
bullet_points: The paper addresses the efficiency bottleneck of long-context inference in Large Language Models (LLMs), particularly during reasoning tasks.
It proposes a novel method, 'Cross-Layer Sparse Attention with Shared Routing,' to mitigate the trade-off between speed and quality in sparse attention mechanisms.
The research aims to improve upon existing sparse attention methods that either sacrifice quality for acceleration or offer limited speedup.

stories.title: Scaffold, Not Vocabulary? A Controlled, Two-Tier, Pre-Registered Study of a Popperian Code-Generation Skill
stories.author: Mehmet Iscan
bullet_points: The study investigates the effectiveness of 'Popperian' prompting skills in improving code generation by Large Language Models (LLMs).
It critically examines the reliance on LLM-as-a-judge systems, noting their inherent biases (positional, self-preference, stylistic).
The research questions whether observed performance gains are due to the prompting skill or artifacts of the evaluation methodology.

stories.title: Latent Reasoning with Normalizing Flows
stories.author: Jiatao Gu
bullet_points: The paper proposes 'Latent Reasoning' as an alternative to explicit Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning in LLMs.
Traditional textual CoT is limited because it forces complex, semantic computation through a discrete, serial token stream.
Latent reasoning aims to provide a higher-bandwidth method for intermediate computation, potentially bypassing the limitations of verbalized steps.

stories.title: USAD 2.0: Scaling Representation Distillation for Universal Audio Understanding
stories.author: James Glass
bullet_points: Introduces USAD 2.0, a new universal audio encoder designed to handle diverse audio inputs for LLMs.
Addresses the limitation of existing domain-specific audio encoders by integrating knowledge from both Self-Supervised Learning (SSL) and supervised methods.
Highlights the growing trend of using a single, unified encoder for various audio tasks within modern AI systems.

stories.title: Revising Context, Shifting Simulated Stance: Auditing LLM-Based Stance Simulation in Online Discussions
stories.author: Jiebo Luo
bullet_points: The research addresses the reliability of using LLMs to simulate user stances in online discussions.
It introduces 'counterfactual context revision' as a method to audit how sensitive LLM simulations are to minor context changes.
The study aims to determine if LLM simulations reflect genuine user beliefs or are artifacts of conversational context.

stories.title: New method turns ocean water into drinking water, without waste
stories.author: speckx
bullet_points: A new method has been developed for converting ocean water into potable drinking water.
The process is highlighted as being waste-free.
The news originates from Hacker News, suggesting a technical or scientific focus.

stories.title: pg_durable: Microsoft open sources in-database durable execution
stories.author: coffeemug
bullet_points: Microsoft has open-sourced 'pg_durable', a feature for in-database durable execution.
This development relates to database reliability and transaction management within PostgreSQL.
The open-sourcing suggests an effort to bring enterprise-grade durability features to the open-source community.

stories.title: Catching the shortcuts AI coding agents take to look done
stories.author: moonrunnerkc
bullet_points: The article discusses the limitations of current AI coding agents.
It highlights that standard linters (Semgrep, ESLint) miss subtle coding flaws.
These missed issues include weak tests, swallowed errors, and incomplete refactoring.

stories.title: Why Coding Stays in Human-AI Collaboration: A Paradox in Stanford's 51 Deployments
stories.author: ikenyal
bullet_points: The article explores the nuanced relationship between human coders and AI tools.
It highlights the contradictory experiences developers have with AI deployment (e.g., 'no results' vs. 'dramatically faster').
The context is drawn from Stanford's deployment of AI across 51 projects, suggesting real-world case studies.

stories.title: How AI Applications Answer From Your Data, Not Their Training
stories.author: abinash1417
bullet_points: The article discusses Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) as a foundational pattern in AI.
RAG allows AI applications to answer questions based on proprietary user data rather than just pre-trained knowledge.
This shift addresses limitations in traditional AI models by grounding responses in specific, external data sources.
